items.briefing:
Security teams are being warned that relying too heavily on AI in security operations centres risks missing real threats, as automated tools can overlook subtle attack signals. Separately, phishing attacks are becoming more sophisticated — scammers are now using campaigns that automatically adjust their appearance based on what device or operating system you're using, making them harder to spot. A technique called "ClickFix" is also surging as a popular way to deliver malware by tricking users into running malicious commands themselves.

**Practical tip:** Be cautious of any unexpected pop-up or webpage asking you to copy and paste a command into your computer — this is a common sign of a ClickFix attack.

featured.title: SMS Sender ID Register goes live to help protect Australians from scams
featured.quote: New SMS Sender ID Register directly protects Australian consumers from scam text message fraud.
